title: Event log append and record writes are not atomic/lock-guarded across processes
description: Audit finding (HIGH). appendEvent uses writeFileSync(flag:'a') which is not guaranteed atomic across processes; two writers can interleave/tear a JSONL line that then fails to parse forever. appendEvent acquires no lock itself, and initLedger never takes withLedgerLock, so the 'single locked write path' invariant is caller-dependent, not structural. writeJsonAtomic uses a fixed `${file}.tmp` (concurrent writers to the same record clobber temp) and does not fsync the file or its directory (rename not durable on crash). Lock stale timeout is an aggressive 15s, which can break mutual exclusion during a slow mutation.
expected: All record/event writes are atomic and mutually exclusive across processes; a torn event line cannot occur.
actual: appendEvent/init can race and interleave; fixed temp name can clobber; no fsync/durability.
next_steps: Route appendEvent (and initLedger writes) through withLedgerLock, or make appendEvent take the lock itself.
Use a unique temp suffix (pid+rand) in writeJsonAtomic; fsync file + dir before/after rename.
Reconsider the 15s stale-lock window for multi-file mutations.
